ideas.title: 10,000 POTA QSOs by Bike: What It Takes to Go the Distance
ideas.why_now: N4JAW just logged their 10,000th POTA contact while activating by bicycle, a remarkable milestone generating community buzz.
ideas.angle: Celebrate the milestone and dig into what sustained POTA dedication looks like — gear, logistics, staying motivated, and combining human-powered travel with ham radio for everyday operators.

ideas.title: POTA and WWFF Finally Making Peace — What It Means for Activators
ideas.why_now: A long-standing code-of-conduct hostility between POTA and WWFF appears to have been formally resolved, opening doors for cross-program cooperation.
ideas.angle: Break down the POTA/WWFF history in plain language and explain practically what the thaw means — can you log contacts for both programs on the same activation? Should you care?

ideas.title: SmartBeaconing Demystified: Dialing In Your APRS Settings
ideas.why_now: A popular community question this week on optimizing SmartBeaconing settings for the Kenwood TH-D75 sparked practical discussion.
ideas.angle: Walk through SmartBeaconing parameters in plain English — speed thresholds, turn sensitivity, time intervals — so everyday operators can stop guessing and actually tune their tracker for how they move.

ideas.title: Tiger Tails, Ground Planes, and Your HT: Easy Antenna Wins
ideas.why_now: A beginner question about tiger tail counterpoise length and SWR tolerance surfaced real confusion that many new HT users share.
ideas.angle: Explain the tiger tail counterpoise clearly — why it helps, how precise you really need to be with the length, and what other low-cost HT antenna improvements everyday operators can make right now.

ideas.title: Reviving Teletext for Ham Radio: Retro Tech or Actual Useful Mode?
ideas.why_now: A Hacker News post on using Teletext in the ham radio context pulled 45 comments and 72 points from a tech-savvy audience.
ideas.angle: Give the curious everyday ham an accessible tour of what Teletext-over-radio actually involves, how it compares to modes like JS8Call or Winlink, and whether it's a fun experiment or something more.

ideas.title: Sporadic-E Season Is Here — How to Chase It and Actually Make Contacts
ideas.why_now: A strong trans-Atlantic sporadic-E opening on 50 MHz and the FM band was reported this week, right in peak Es season.
ideas.angle: Give operators a practical seasonal playbook — what bands to watch, how to use propagation tools in real time, and what to do the moment an opening appears so you don't miss it.

ideas.title: QSL Cards and ARRL Membership: What Beginners Actually Need to Know
ideas.why_now: A self-described remedial question about QSL cards and ARRL membership drew genuine community engagement and represents a gap many newcomers share.
ideas.angle: Cut through the confusion with a friendly, no-shame explainer — Logbook of the World, eQSL, paper cards, and what ARRL membership does and doesn't get you as an everyday operator.

ideas.title: I Like Radio, Not Radio People — Making Club Life Work for Introverts
ideas.why_now: A candid Mastodon post about dreading local club culture despite loving the hobby resonated with a relatable tension many hams feel.
ideas.angle: Address the 'I love ham radio but clubs feel unwelcoming' experience directly — share strategies for finding your people, what good clubs look like, and how to get value from club membership on your own terms.
